metadata.generatedFrom: Itotori roadmap after encrypted-engine and runtime-port audit pass
metadata.currentBaseline: DB-backed fixture hello world across Itotori, Kaifuu, and Utsushi

priorityDefinitions.P0: Critical orchestration, data integrity, or alpha-readiness blocker. A failed P0 audit blocks merge and must be repaired immediately.
priorityDefinitions.P1: Required for alpha readiness or for the owning spec acceptance criteria. P1 audit findings block merge for the owning spec.
priorityDefinitions.P2: Important quality, scale, or continuous expansion work. P2 findings may become new DAG nodes.
priorityDefinitions.P3: Exploratory, nice-to-have, or research work. P3 findings should become DAG nodes or be attached to existing planned nodes unless already covered by the active node scope.

statusDefinitions.complete: Already implemented and verified in main.
statusDefinitions.planned: Not started. It becomes ready when all dependencies are complete.
statusDefinitions.in_progress: Owned by an active branch or worktree.
statusDefinitions.blocked: Cannot proceed without a named decision or external input.

nodes.title: DB-backed monorepo hello world
nodes.summary: Current committed baseline: fixture extraction, deterministic draft, patch, delta package, runtime smoke report, DB-backed dashboard status, local and GitHub CI.
acceptanceCriteria: GitHub CI is green
GitHub Hello World workflow is green
Local just hello prints status=hello_world_passed
auditFocus: Baseline must not claim real engine support

nodes.title: Alpha localization project readiness definition
nodes.summary: Define the exact feature set, engine/readiness set, fixture requirements, dashboard flows, quality/cost reports, non-goals, readiness checks, and demo script required before starting the first real localization project.
acceptanceCriteria: Alpha engine/readiness set and fixture requirements are explicit
Required dashboard workflows, real-provider proof, and quality/cost reports are named
Known non-goals are documented without implying the monorepo enters a terminal product state
auditFocus: Benchmarking or real-LLM proof deferred until full-game testing

nodes.title: Runtime probe environment and fidelity policy
nodes.summary: Define static-vs-runtime evidence tiers, screenshot capability matrix, OS/browser/Wine assumptions, artifact limits, and wording that prevents static analysis pretending to be runtime.
acceptanceCriteria: Evidence tier is visible everywhere evidence is shown
OS/native/Wine/browser assumptions are explicit
Static analysis, runtime trace, screenshot capture, and VM playback are distinct

nodes.title: Product workflow and HITL UX spec
nodes.summary: Specify the human interaction points, decision queue information architecture, style-guide conversation, batch review model, and escalation policy before building UX-heavy flows.
acceptanceCriteria: Random line without context is explicitly rejected
Reviewers see source, draft, context, screenshots/evidence, and reasoning together
Human escalation is reserved for style disputes and high-value ambiguity

nodes.title: Engine fixture sourcing and reference implementation policy
nodes.summary: Define how engine fixtures and reference implementations are sourced, licensed, documented, and used by parallel adapter agents.
acceptanceCriteria: Every engine adapter has documented fixture source and support boundary
Reference implementation licenses are reviewed before porting
Unsupported encrypted or compiled variants fail with semantic capability errors

nodes.title: Localization surface inventory and contract ADR
nodes.summary: Define the canonical inventory of game localization surfaces and the contract source of truth before expanding schemas.
acceptanceCriteria: Dialogue, narration, speakers, choices, UI, tutorials, bestiary/database text, song titles, images-with-text, metadata, control markup, variables, ruby/furigana, and romanized/non-translated cases are covered
TS schema, JSON Schema, Rust serde, and generated binding responsibilities are decided
The inventory is engine-agnostic

nodes.title: Machine-readable spec DAG and orchestration docs
nodes.summary: Introduce the canonical roadmap DAG artifact, validation command, ready-node popper, and docs for the plan/implement/audit/merge lifecycle.
acceptanceCriteria: DAG validates structurally
Ready nodes can be listed deterministically
Docs define P0/P1 audit gating and P2/P3 reinsertion
auditFocus: Initial ready set enables parallel work

nodes.title: GitHub issue sync for DAG nodes
nodes.summary: Add a deterministic command that creates or updates GitHub issues from DAG nodes, preserving labels, dependencies, status, and acceptance criteria.
acceptanceCriteria: Dry-run output is stable
Existing issues are updated rather than duplicated
Every synced issue links back to its DAG node id
Issue sync is non-mutating by default; GitHub writes require an explicit apply flag

verification.value: Unit tests for issue body rendering

auditFocus: Issue text remains useful to implementation agents
Accidental issue mutation from a default command

nodes.title: Agent worktree lifecycle playbook
nodes.summary: Define and script the branch/worktree lifecycle used by the orchestrator: claim node, plan, implement, audit, repair, merge, and mark complete.
acceptanceCriteria: A coding agent can follow the lifecycle without hidden context
P0/P1 repair loops are explicitly documented
P2/P3 findings have DAG insertion rules
auditFocus: No process gaps around failed audits

nodes.title: Affected task detection and CI cache hardening
nodes.summary: Make CI fast and trustworthy for parallel work by formalizing affected-package detection, Vite+ cache usage, Rust cache usage, and required gates per project.
acceptanceCriteria: A docs-only PR skips expensive runtime checks safely
A schema change fans out to all dependent checks
CI still has one full-suite path before merge

verification.value: GitHub Actions dry run through a PR

auditFocus: CI results matching local just recipes

nodes.title: Golden fixture and private corpus policy
nodes.summary: Define how public fixtures, private purchased-game fixtures, hashes, metrics, and reproducible benchmark summaries are organized without redistributing copyrighted content.
acceptanceCriteria: Public CI can run with only open fixtures
Private local corpora can run without changing committed paths
Benchmark reports can cite hashes and aggregate stats
auditFocus: No copyrighted raw assets in repo

nodes.title: Spec audit templates and finding schema
nodes.summary: Standardize audit output so review agents can report P0-P3 findings that are machine-ingestible back into the DAG.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit output is parseable JSON plus human summary
P0/P1 findings block completion
P2/P3 findings can be converted into new node drafts

nodes.title: Toolchain version and upgrade policy
nodes.summary: Document and enforce how latest Node, pnpm, Vite+, TypeScript, Rust, dependency upgrades, lockfiles, and CI upgrade checks are managed.
acceptanceCriteria: Latest-and-greatest policy is explicit
just upgrade remains the canonical update command
CI failures after upgrades have a documented repair path

nodes.title: BDD and fixture-driven testing standard
nodes.summary: Define behavior-first testing conventions for TypeScript, Rust, DB, MSW, golden fixtures, property tests, mutation tests, and no-live-API CI.
acceptanceCriteria: Given/When/Then style is defined where useful
MSW and DB test isolation expectations are explicit
Live provider calls are opt-in and never required in CI
auditFocus: Tests that only assert implementation details

nodes.title: Orchestrator claim, plan, audit lifecycle CLI
nodes.summary: Implement the DAG orchestration lifecycle as tooling: claim nodes, create worktrees, ingest audit reports, manage P0/P1 repair loops, and draft P2/P3 follow-up nodes.
acceptanceCriteria: Two agents cannot claim the same node
P0/P1 audit findings keep the node in repair state
P2/P3 findings can draft new DAG nodes without hand-copying

nodes.title: Large-project synthetic scale harness
nodes.summary: Prove import, indexing, batch planning, queueing, and dashboard queries against generated large bridge bundles before real huge games force redesign.
acceptanceCriteria: A synthetic 1M+ character project imports and indexes within documented limits
Batch planning has a measured timing budget
Dashboard status queries have explicit performance expectations

nodes.title: Mutation and property-test quality gates
nodes.summary: Add explicit mutation and property-test commands for the highest-risk TypeScript and Rust packages.
deliverables: Vitest mutation-survivor guard in packages/localization-bridge-schema/test/schema.test.ts for schema, delta, protected-span, and permission invariants
proptest-based Rust property tests in crates/kaifuu-core for patch compatibility and protected-span preservation
Documented threshold constants in packages/localization-bridge-schema/test/mutation-thresholds.ts and crates/kaifuu-core/src/contracts.rs
Local quality recipes `pnpm exec vitest run packages/localization-bridge-schema/test/schema.test.ts` and `cargo test -p kaifuu-core property`
acceptanceCriteria: Selected schema, delta, and protected-span logic has mutation or property coverage
The Vitest mutation-survivor guard rejects at least one committed invalid fixture per selected invariant
Rust property tests run with fixed public seeds and bounded case counts documented in the test module
Mutation/property failures produce actionable diagnostics

nodes.title: Harden exact upgrade recipe verification
nodes.summary: Harden the toolchain policy verifier so exact just upgrade command comparisons cannot silently accept ignored-error prefixes such as -corepack use pnpm@latest.
deliverables: Negative verifier fixture for a just upgrade command with a leading - prefix
Verifier diagnostics that name ignored-error prefixes when exact upgrade commands are checked
acceptanceCriteria: The verifier rejects or explicitly flags exact upgrade commands that use just ignored-error prefixes
A fixture containing -corepack use pnpm@latest fails verification instead of passing after prefix stripping
The current canonical just upgrade recipe remains accepted without ignored-error prefixes

nodes.title: Self-contained DB test and scale smoke recipe
nodes.summary: Add alpha-required local and CI-friendly database test recipes using the existing Itotori DB package and scale harness, with deterministic skip artifacts when a database URL is unavailable.
deliverables: `just db-up`, `just db-wait`, `just db-migrate`, `just db-reset`, `just ci-itotori`, and `just itotori-scale-smoke` recipe contract
`DATABASE_URL` and `ITOTORI_SCALE_SCHEMA` environment-variable documentation for local and CI runs
Generated smoke summary at `.tmp/itotori-scale-harness/smoke/summary.json` from `scripts/itotori-scale-harness.mjs --profile smoke`
No-database skip report at `.tmp/itotori-db/no-database-skipped.json` with status, reason, command, checkedEnv, and timestamp fields
Contributor docs naming the disposable Postgres path and the exact commands that feed alpha readiness
acceptanceCriteria: `just db-up && just db-wait && just db-migrate && just db-reset` provisions or resets an isolated Postgres database using `DATABASE_URL` without depending on a developer's existing schema

When `DATABASE_URL` is unset for the DB-only verification path, the workflow exits successfully, prints `itotori db tests skipped: DATABASE_URL unset`, and writes `.tmp/itotori-db/no-database-skipped.json`
`pnpm --filter @itotori/db test` and `pnpm --filter @itotori/app test` run under `just ci-itotori` against the documented disposable database

`just itotori-scale-smoke` runs `node scripts/itotori-scale-harness.mjs --profile smoke` and produces `.tmp/itotori-scale-harness/smoke/summary.json` with profile, outputPath, budgetPassed, unitCount, batchCount, scheduledJobCount, and schemaKept
Docs name `DATABASE_URL`, `ITOTORI_SCALE_SCHEMA`, `COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME`, and the no-secret public CI defaults
auditFocus: DB tests that depend on hidden local state
Required scale smoke docs without a runnable CI-safe target
Shared tooling that leaks implementation-specific setup

nodes.title: Local Node toolchain warning cleanup
nodes.summary: Clean up local Node >=24.14.0 toolchain warnings so contributors see actionable diagnostics instead of confusing version noise.
deliverables: Toolchain warning regression fixture or reproduction case
acceptanceCriteria: Local checks do not emit avoidable Node >=24.14.0 warnings when the configured runtime is correct
When the runtime is too old, the warning names the required version and the policy source
The cleanup does not weaken the latest-and-greatest toolchain policy
auditFocus: Noisy local warnings hiding real failures
Weakening toolchain enforcement to silence warnings

nodes.title: Canonical research-to-DAG crosswalk
nodes.summary: Mark research documents as historical evidence or current planning input and map adopted proposals to live DAG node ids so planning agents do not duplicate stale recommendations.
deliverables: Research-to-DAG crosswalk document or generated section
acceptanceCriteria: Research docs state whether they are historical evidence or current planning input
Every adopted proposal maps to live node ids or is explicitly marked superseded
Canonical planning docs avoid MVP, pre-MVP, and post-MVP framing except in historical notes
Stale absent-from-roadmap claims are removed or marked superseded
auditFocus: Duplicate DAG nodes from historical proposals
MVP framing drifting back into canonical docs

nodes.title: Roadmap verification semantics and placeholder validator
nodes.summary: Validator detects runnable verification, concrete deliverables, non-placeholder acceptance, and integration nodes naming exact surfaces.
acceptanceCriteria: Every non-docs-only planned implementation node has at least one runnable command verification entry
Manual review, manual smoke, and fixture review entries are rejected when they are the only evidence for tests or smoke behavior
Generic deliverables such as implementation, fixtures, tests, regression coverage, and end-to-end fixture are rejected unless they name concrete surfaces or artifacts
Placeholder acceptance such as has concrete executable behavior or schema validation is rejected with the offending field name
Meta follow-up packs, decision nodes, feasibility nodes, and normalize-later nodes are rejected unless their status is cancelled
Integration nodes must name the exact composed surfaces, artifacts, commands, or adapters they wire together
Validator errors identify the node id, field, and offending value that needs repair
auditFocus: Integration nodes without exact composed surfaces
Validator errors too vague to route to an agent

nodes.title: Bridge schema v0.2 domain model
nodes.summary: Expand the bridge schema from fixture dialogue to game-localization primitives: assets, text surfaces, choices, UI, images-with-text, speaker metadata, source revisions, route context, and protected spans.
acceptanceCriteria: No raw string categories where enums are known
UUID7 ids are required and validated
Schema distinguishes parser-unknown from reader-unknown speaker state

nodes.title: Bridge contract validation
nodes.summary: Make TypeScript and Rust agree on bridge bundle JSON through shared fixtures and strict validation before patch, runtime, benchmark, and finding contracts are layered on.
acceptanceCriteria: Every committed bridge fixture validates in TypeScript and Rust
Invalid bridge fixtures fail with semantic errors
Bridge schema version behavior is documented

nodes.title: Multi-surface public bridge fixture corpus
nodes.summary: Replace the single-line fixture with public bridge fixtures covering dialogue, names, choices, UI labels, tutorial text, glossary terms, protected markup, and image-text metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: At least five text surfaces are represented
Protected spans include placeholders and inline control markup
Fixture supports 1->N locale branches
Patch exports and runtime reports are handled by later full-system artifact nodes

nodes.title: Patch and source revision semantics
nodes.summary: Define how source game versions, source text changes, patch exports, patch results, and delta patch packages relate across reruns and updated games.
acceptanceCriteria: A source typo patch can invalidate only affected units
Patch application reports incompatible source hashes clearly
Delta packages can be traced to a source revision

nodes.title: Runtime evidence schema v0.2
nodes.summary: Define trace, branch, capture, recording, approximation, and validation finding schemas for Utsushi-generated evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Evidence can be linked back to bridge units
Approximation tiers are explicit
Screenshots and recordings are referenced, not embedded by default

nodes.title: Benchmark and cost report schema
nodes.summary: Define benchmark run, model/provider, cost, token, quality finding, deterministic QA, and human evaluation result schemas.
acceptanceCriteria: Reports capture exact model, provider, prompt preset, and timestamp
MTL baseline runs fit the same schema
QA agent evaluation is first-class

nodes.title: Event and finding taxonomy
nodes.summary: Define canonical event, task, finding, severity, provenance, and causality records for event-driven triage across the suite.
acceptanceCriteria: A QA finding can point to source annotation, style guide, model output, or patching cause
P0-P3 severities are distinct from localization quality categories
Events are append-only

nodes.title: Permission model and local-user baseline
nodes.summary: Introduce permission-based access primitives now, with a single all-permissions local user for alpha readiness and a clean path to custom roles later.
acceptanceCriteria: No role strings are hardcoded into authorization checks
Every API mutation has a permission gate
alpha local mode remains frictionless

nodes.title: Localization quality taxonomy and benchmark protocol
nodes.summary: Choose and document the localization quality taxonomy and benchmark protocol, including MQM comparison, game-localization extensions, seeded defects, and QA-agent metrics.
acceptanceCriteria: Localization quality severities are distinct from DAG P0-P3
QA agents map findings to the taxonomy
Benchmark reports distinguish severity, category, root cause, and reviewer adjudication
auditFocus: Conflating orchestration severity with translation severity

nodes.title: Full contract validation
nodes.summary: Validate bridge, patch, delta, runtime, benchmark, asset-policy, and finding contracts together across TypeScript and Rust.
acceptanceCriteria: Every committed contract fixture validates in TypeScript and Rust
Invalid fixtures fail with semantic errors
Schema compatibility across the suite is documented
Permission and local-user contract fixtures validate with the rest of the suite

nodes.title: Asset localization policy schema
nodes.summary: Represent non-dialogue localization decisions for images with text, UI textures, audio/song metadata, fonts, credits, videos, and romanize/translate/do-not-translate choices.
acceptanceCriteria: Image text, song titles, UI art, fonts, credits, and videos have representable policy choices
Policies can be locale-branch scoped
Metadata-first support does not imply OCR or image editing is complete
auditFocus: Policy not tied to locale branches

nodes.title: Full-system golden artifact corpus
nodes.summary: Add expected patch exports, patch results, delta packages, runtime reports, benchmark reports, and findings for public fixtures after their schemas exist.
acceptanceCriteria: Full-system fixtures cover success and failure cases
Artifacts cite fixture hashes and schema versions
Fixtures are public-safe and reproducible

nodes.title: Permission-gate negative test matrix
nodes.summary: Expand authorization denial coverage from the current single repository mutation check to every mutation protected by the permission model.
deliverables: Negative authorization tests for saveDrafts, savePatchExport, and saveRuntimeReport
Shared helper for asserting denied repository/API mutations
acceptanceCriteria: Every current permission-gated mutation has a test proving a user without the required permission is denied
Tests cover reset, saveDrafts, savePatchExport, saveRuntimeReport, and any other mutation gates present when the node is implemented
The matrix names each mutation, required permission, success fixture, and denial fixture
Adding a new permission-gated mutation without denial coverage fails a targeted test or checklist
auditFocus: Only happy-path permission checks are tested
A newly added mutation bypasses denial coverage
Repository and API authorization behavior diverge

nodes.title: Permission constant and migration drift guard
nodes.summary: Prevent permission values from drifting between TypeScript constants and SQL migration check constraints by generating or verifying their consistency.
deliverables: Generated SQL permission constraint or consistency verification script
acceptanceCriteria: There is one documented source of truth for permission values
SQL migration constraints are generated from or automatically checked against the TypeScript permission constants
A permission added to TypeScript without the corresponding migration constraint update fails verification
Contributor docs describe the required workflow for adding, renaming, or retiring permissions
auditFocus: Permission value drift between code and migrations
Manual SQL check constraints missing new permissions
Hardening that rewrites historical migrations unsafely

nodes.title: Unified relative path validator
nodes.summary: Unify profile path validation and package-controlled write path validation around one shared relative-path rule so dot, traversal, absolute, drive-prefix, and separator edge cases cannot diverge.
deliverables: Profile validation migration to the shared validator
Package-controlled write migration to the shared validator
Negative fixture matrix for dot and traversal components
acceptanceCriteria: Profile validation rejects . components consistently with package-controlled writes
safe_join_relative and profile validation share a documented rule or one implementation
Absolute, drive-prefix, empty, dot, and parent traversal paths have regression fixtures
The shared validator documents whether it only validates paths or returns normalized safe output
auditFocus: Dot components accepted by one surface only

nodes.title: Branch-event multi-ref DB regression
nodes.summary: Strengthen the DB branch-event persistence test with distinct labelBridgeUnitRef and targetBridgeUnitRef bridge-unit references to prove aggregation records every branch option reference.
deliverables: Repository assertion for aggregated branch refs
Regression test covering multi-ref branch options
acceptanceCriteria: A branch event option with different labelBridgeUnitRef and targetBridgeUnitRef values persists both references
The DB test fails if aggregation collapses multiple bridge-unit refs into one value
The fixture names the label and target refs clearly enough to diagnose regressions
auditFocus: Branch ref aggregation hiding missing refs
Label and target refs accidentally identical

nodes.title: Benchmark QA evaluated-system validation
nodes.summary: Tighten benchmark report validation so llm_qa provider runs and findings are covered by QA-agent evaluations for the same evaluatedSystemId instead of only globally.
deliverables: Mismatched llm_qa and QA-agent negative fixtures
Report validator assertions for provider runs and findings
acceptanceCriteria: Every llm_qa provider run is covered by at least one QA-agent evaluation with the same evaluatedSystemId
llm_qa findings cannot satisfy QA-agent coverage for a different evaluatedSystemId
Reports with only global QA-agent coverage fail semantic validation
A valid fixture proves multiple evaluated systems can coexist with separate QA-agent coverage
auditFocus: Global QA coverage masking per-system gaps

nodes.title: Alpha vertical proof manifest contract
nodes.summary: Define one alpha proof manifest that ties public fixture identity, engine profile, source revision, bridge units, runtime targets, patch/export/result artifacts, provider proof, benchmark outputs, and content hashes across the suite.
deliverables: Manifest validator command for fixture, engine, bridge, runtime, provider, benchmark, and artifact hashes
acceptanceCriteria: One manifest record ties fixture id, engine profile, source revision, bridge unit ids, runtime target ids, patch export ids, patch result ids, provider proof ids, benchmark ids, and content hashes.
Provider proof ids are non-empty and must reference a sanitized provider proof bundle emitted by ALPHA-008 or ITOTORI-116.

Manifest validation fails when any Kaifuu extraction or patch artifact, Itotori export or result artifact, Utsushi runtime evidence id, provider proof id, benchmark id, or hash is missing or mismatched.
Public fixtures contain only redistributable ids, hashes, and redacted artifact paths; raw prompts, responses, private asset paths, and source text are excluded.
Alpha vertical commands can emit and revalidate the manifest without live provider credentials or private corpus inputs.
auditFocus: Vertical proof artifacts that cannot be traced across projects
Hash or fixture identity drift between generated reports
Provider or runtime proof referenced only in prose
Private text or raw provider payload leakage

nodes.title: Patch asset-kind coverage matrix
nodes.summary: Parameterize font-substitution wrong-kind patchRef coverage across image, audio, and video patch asset kinds instead of exercising only the image case.
acceptanceCriteria: Wrong-kind patchRef tests run for image, audio, and video patch asset kinds
Each covered kind has a positive fixture and at least one negative wrong-kind fixture
The parameterized suite fails if validation is accidentally limited to image patch refs
auditFocus: Wrong-kind patch refs accepted for audio or video

nodes.title: RFC3339 timestamp validation parity
nodes.summary: Make Rust and TypeScript contract validators reject and accept the same RFC3339 timestamp forms across shared fixture contracts.
acceptanceCriteria: Rust and TypeScript validators agree on valid RFC3339 timestamps
Both validators reject malformed, ambiguous, or unsupported timestamp forms with semantic errors
The parity matrix covers offsets, fractional seconds, UTC Z notation, and obvious invalid dates
Existing contract fixtures are updated only where they violate the documented rule
auditFocus: Fixture updates that hide real validation mismatches

nodes.title: Bridge surface normalization semantics
nodes.summary: Preserve expanded bridge text surface identity and protected-span semantics through shared and Itotori normalization paths.
acceptanceCriteria: Expanded bridge text surface kinds survive normalization without being collapsed into generic dialogue
Protected spans keep offsets, identifiers, and semantic meaning through shared and Itotori import paths
Fixtures cover UI labels, choices, tutorial text, glossary terms, protected markup, and image-text metadata
Normalization failures report semantic diagnostics instead of silently dropping surface metadata
auditFocus: Expanded text surfaces collapsed during normalization
Itotori import behavior diverging from shared contracts

nodes.title: Queue index migration drift guard
nodes.summary: Add a focused guard that keeps Drizzle schema index definitions and SQL migration indexes aligned for event outbox and job queue tables.
deliverables: Regression fixture for event queue indexes
Contributor workflow for queue index changes
acceptanceCriteria: Queue indexes declared in code and migrations are verified by an automated check
The check fails when a migration omits a required event queue index
Contributor instructions describe how to update code and migrations together
auditFocus: Drizzle schema and migration index drift
Missing queue performance indexes after schema edits

nodes.title: Public golden corpus regeneration utility
nodes.summary: Commit a deterministic utility for regenerating the public full-system golden corpus without relying on private fixtures or one-off local commands.
deliverables: Fixture hash and schema manifest update
Contributor documentation for intentional golden changes
acceptanceCriteria: Public full-system goldens can be regenerated deterministically from committed inputs
A check mode fails when regenerated artifacts differ from committed goldens
Fixture hashes and schema versions are updated together with regenerated artifacts
The utility does not require private corpus files or live provider credentials

nodes.title: en-US full-system artifact goldens
nodes.summary: Add en-US full-system artifact goldens alongside the existing target locale so public fixtures prove locale-branch behavior across at least two concrete target locales.
deliverables: en-US delta package and runtime report goldens
Golden validation command coverage for both target locales
acceptanceCriteria: en-US artifacts are generated from public-safe fixtures and committed with fixture hashes
Locale-specific branch metadata is visible in the golden artifacts
The corpus makes locale assumptions explicit instead of treating one target locale as universal
Both locale sets validate without live provider credentials
Golden regeneration fails when either target locale is missing expected patch, delta, runtime, benchmark, or finding artifacts
auditFocus: Golden corpus bloat without regression value

nodes.title: Round-trip report JSON schema
nodes.summary: Formalize the Kaifuu round-trip golden harness report as a versioned JSON schema with positive and negative fixtures.
acceptanceCriteria: Round-trip report artifacts declare the schema version they satisfy
Valid fixture reports pass schema validation without depending on adapter-specific assumptions
Invalid report fixtures fail with diagnostics that name the missing or malformed field
The schema can evolve without breaking existing v0.2 compatibility checks silently
auditFocus: Report fixtures drifting from generated output
Schema fields that encode fixture-engine assumptions

nodes.title: Catalog schema and provenance model
nodes.summary: Promote work identity, external ids, source provenance, language status, releases, local scans, and seed targets into first-class typed Postgres state instead of local research scripts.
deliverables: Drizzle schema and migrations for work identity and provenance
Fixture records for VNDB, EGS, DLsite, Steam, IGDB, Wikidata, and local corpus provenance
acceptanceCriteria: Catalog identity does not depend on VNDB as the root source
External ids, source provenance, fetched-at metadata, conflict records, releases, and local scan references are repeatable records rather than one-column shortcuts
Permissions and local-user defaults cover catalog reads and writes without bypassing the existing authorization model
Fixtures prove DLsite-only, EGS-only, VNDB-linked, Steam-linked, IGDB-linked, and local-only works can coexist

nodes.title: Cross-source resolver fixture command
nodes.summary: Implement the catalog resolver command that composes exact external-id linking, fuzzy candidate generation, and conflict review into one recorded-fixture artifact.
deliverables: Resolver artifact `artifacts/catalog/resolver-integration.json` with exact links, fuzzy candidates, and conflict records
Recorded fixtures for exact match, fuzzy-only candidate, manual conflict, and no-match diagnostics
API/read-model test proving resolver output is reviewable without live network access
acceptanceCriteria: The resolver command emits exact-link ids, fuzzy candidate ids, conflict ids, source registry ids, and provenance hashes in one schema-valid artifact.
Exact-link, fuzzy-candidate, conflict, and no-match paths are all fixture-tested.
Malformed or unsupported source payloads return semantic diagnostics rather than hidden success states.
Resolver output never requires live catalog network access in public CI.
auditFocus: Integration shell hiding new implementation work

nodes.title: Translation completeness intelligence
nodes.summary: Define the recorded-source registry, language-completeness schema, and conflict-safe evidence model used by source importers and benchmark selection without implementing source-specific adapters.
deliverables: Language status enum and provenance schema
acceptanceCriteria: Language status supports official_full, fan_full, fan_partial, mtl, interface_only, unverified_console, none, unknown, and conflict evidence
Recorded facts preserve source id, source kind, source version, fetched/imported-at metadata, parser version, confidence, and raw-public-vs-private redaction class
Multiple recorded sources and local sidecars can disagree without overwriting one another or requiring a source-specific adapter in this node
MTL-only, fan-partial, no-English, unknown, and conflict fixture sets can be queried as benchmark or opportunity pools from normalized recorded facts
Public reports use aggregate counts and source ids without publishing private local corpus content
auditFocus: Source-specific adapter behavior hidden in the framework node
MTL and partial statuses collapsed into full translations
Recorded-source provenance or redaction class lost

nodes.title: Local corpus scanner and redacted sidecar writer
nodes.summary: Turn the private local game library scan into a first-class local workflow that records owned corpus inventory, engine evidence, archive/install state, and redacted sidecars without committing private assets.
acceptanceCriteria: The scanner distinguishes source archives, installed games, collection members, editions, and sidecar metadata
Sidecars record stable local ids, owned flag, engine/readiness evidence, aggregate counts, hashes, and tool versions without raw text, private paths, keys, screenshots, or story filenames
Sidecars emit versioned engine evidence compatible with current Kaifuu detection output
Capability merge into catalog readiness records is owned by KAIFUU-053 and CATALOG-007
Absence of private local corpora never affects public CI
auditFocus: Engine evidence drift from Kaifuu detection

nodes.title: Benchmark seed finder
nodes.summary: Expose readiness-aware benchmark seed queries from catalog, local corpus, alpha recorded importer evidence, engine readiness, and existing translation status so alpha benchmark and quality runs can select credible corpora.
acceptanceCriteria: Benchmark seeds distinguish local ownership, translation completeness, CATALOG-011 recorded importer provenance, DLsite demand evidence, engine readiness, and benchmark usefulness
Seed records distinguish identify, inventory, extract, patch, helper, and runtime capability levels instead of flattening engine readiness into one boolean
MTL-only, fan-partial, no-English, conflict, demand-ranked, and readiness-filtered pools can be selected from public fixtures, recorded importer fixtures, and private-local aggregate sidecars
Benchmark seed output can be generated without leaking private paths, filenames, story text, or raw corpus records
The cost and quality benchmark flow can select corpora by capability level, readiness, demand bucket, and translation completeness instead of arbitrary fixture availability
auditFocus: Alpha benchmark selection bypassing recorded importer evidence

nodes.title: Opportunity ranking service
nodes.summary: Add opportunity ranking for localization project selection from completeness, local corpus, DLsite demand, platform-language conflicts, market prevalence, engine readiness, existing translation status, and benchmark usefulness.
acceptanceCriteria: RPG Maker MV/MZ, RGSS3/VX Ace, KiriKiri XP3, TyranoScript, Unity, Siglus, Wolf, MTL-only, fan-partial, no-English, official-English-conflict, and demand-ranked pools can be ranked with explainable evidence
Opportunity output distinguishes prevalence, demand, local ownership, translation completeness, adapter readiness, runtime evidence readiness, benchmark usefulness, and unknown evidence
DLsite sales, rating, wishlist, rank, and work_type facts influence ranking through explicit weights or rule explanations rather than hidden heuristics
Platform-language conflicts can demote false untranslated candidates before benchmark or alpha project selection
Ranking can be generated from public fixtures and private-local aggregate sidecars without leaking private data

nodes.title: Crawler job and checkpoint framework
nodes.summary: Implement the resumable crawler job substrate, checkpoint model, rate-limit metadata, and recorded-fixture execution path used by source-specific importers.
acceptanceCriteria: Crawler jobs resume from checkpoints without duplicating imported facts

Source-specific parsing for VNDB, EGS, DLsite, Steam, IGDB, and Wikidata is delegated to separate adapter nodes

nodes.title: Edition and milestone mapping model

DLsite translation parent/child and VNDB milestone data can inform whether an existing translation is portable

nodes.title: MV/MZ local corpus readiness integration

Readiness output distinguishes public fixture support from private-local aggregate evidence
No private paths, filenames, raw text, keys, or screenshots are persisted to committed paths

Public and private evidence collapsed together

nodes.title: Kaifuu adapter trait and game profile model

acceptanceCriteria: Fixture engine uses the same trait as real engines

nodes.title: Protected markup parser and span normalizer

nodes.title: Engine detection and profile CLI

acceptanceCriteria: Fixture adapter passes a snapshot round-trip test against the production surface (not an in-test FixtureInspectable).
Existing fixture trace, capture, and smoke_validate goldens continue to pass.
utsushi-cli runtime registry compiles and runs unchanged.
auditFocus: Fixture inspect/restore implementation matches the substrate's Inspectable/Restorable contract verbatim (no silent best-effort branch).
Round-trip determinism holds against the production fixture surface, not just an in-test stub.
No regression in existing trace, capture, or smoke_validate goldens.

nodes.title: Snapshot replay-tail integration test against ReplayLog
nodes.summary: Strengthen the round-trip determinism claim by exercising a real ReplayLog through the snapshot/restore/diff substrate, replacing the placeholder observe() helper in tests/snapshot_round_trip.rs with a log-driven flow that asserts observation-sequence equality through the replay infrastructure.
deliverables: Integration test constructing a ReplayLog with at least two ReplayEntry records
Test driving the fixture from the log, snapshotting at a known tick, mutating, restoring, and replaying the tail
Byte-equality assertion on the observation sequence collected through the replay infrastructure
acceptanceCriteria: ReplayLog is constructed and driven explicitly (no ad-hoc observation helper).
Snapshot restore at tick T followed by tail replay produces an observation sequence equal to the pre-mutation from-zero replay tail.
Test is hermetic — no host paths, no clock side effects, no external fixtures.
auditFocus: ReplayLog is the actual source of observations (not a stub).
Tail replay starts at the snapshot's anchor tick, not from zero.
Observation sequence equality is byte-level, not field-level.

nodes.title: Direct positive-rejection test for snapshot 16 KiB ceiling
nodes.summary: Add a direct positive-rejection test for `SnapshotError::SnapshotTooLarge` covering the 16 KiB serialized-snapshot ceiling enforcement path in `Snapshot::validate`. The current test surface only asserts that a clean snapshot stays under the ceiling; the rejection branch itself is not directly exercised (the state-tree ceiling test reaches the same JSON-size-rejection mechanism on a sibling path).
deliverables: Unit test under crates/utsushi-core/src/snapshot/snapshot.rs constructing a Snapshot whose serialized form exceeds SNAPSHOT_MAX_SERIALIZED_BYTES
Assertion that SnapshotError::SnapshotTooLarge is returned with size > ceiling
acceptanceCriteria: Test exercises the SnapshotError::SnapshotTooLarge rejection branch in Snapshot::validate directly.
Constant SNAPSHOT_MAX_SERIALIZED_BYTES is referenced from the test so a future ceiling change updates both the validator and the test together.
auditFocus: Test asserts the rejection branch, not just that a clean snapshot is under the ceiling.
Constant SNAPSHOT_MAX_SERIALIZED_BYTES is referenced from the test.

nodes.title: MaxMismatchesPerEvent truncation cap for trace conformance
nodes.summary: Add an opt-in per-event truncation cap to `TraceCheckOptions` so production engine-port runs against large traces emit bounded mismatch lists without losing the exhaustive default that UTSUSHI-027's alpha-track fixtures rely on.
deliverables: `TraceMismatchKind::TruncatedPerEvent` typed sentinel that carries the suppressed-count detail
Unit + integration tests for the unbounded and bounded paths
acceptanceCriteria: Setting `max_mismatches_per_event = None` reproduces the current exhaustive emission (regression-tested).
Setting `max_mismatches_per_event = Some(N)` caps per-event emissions at N and appends exactly one `TruncatedPerEvent` sentinel when suppression occurred.
The new code is in `codes::ALL` and the mod.rs parity test exhaustively covers it.
auditFocus: Truncation observability via the typed sentinel kind
Codes-registry parity for the new code

nodes.title: Optional bridge-unit-id normalisation modes for trace conformance
nodes.summary: Add an opt-in normalisation mode to bridge-unit-id equality so engine adapters that emit stable UUIDs in different surface forms (uppercase vs lowercase hex) do not trip spurious BridgeUnitDivergent mismatches.
deliverables: `BridgeUnitIdNormalisation` enum with `Exact` (default) and at least one folding variant
Symmetric application in the per-event comparison loop
acceptanceCriteria: Default (`Exact`) reproduces UTSUSHI-027's byte-for-byte behaviour.
`LowercaseHex` folds both sides before comparison; UUIDv7 inputs with case-only differences match.
The new option is plumbed through the fixture loader (`TraceFixtureOptions`).
auditFocus: Wire-shape impact on the fixture JSON (additive only)

nodes.title: Real terminal-bridge-unit-id evidence on branch conformance Pass
nodes.summary: Replace the UTSUSHI-027 placeholder where branch-conformance Pass evidence cites `branch_id` as a `bridge_unit_id` with a real `terminal_bridge_unit_id` field on `GoldenBranch` / `ObservedBranch`, so ingestion can join discovered branches to their terminal bridge units without inferring identity from the discovery key.
deliverables: `terminal_bridge_unit_id: String` field on both golden and observed branch types
Updated `build_branch_evidence` to cite the new field
Fixture corpus updated (additive `terminalBridgeUnitId` on every branch)
Tests asserting the evidence ref carries the terminal id, not the branch id
acceptanceCriteria: The new field is required at construction with the shared id-validation rules.
`BranchCheckResult::Pass.evidence_refs` carries `terminal_bridge_unit_id` values that are distinct from `branch_id` in at least one positive fixture.
UTSUSHI-030 round-trip parity (when it lands) sees the new field through the JSON wire shape.
auditFocus: Schema impact (additive field, no version bump)
Test coverage for the distinct-id case

nodes.title: Typed ConformanceError for inadmissible evidence-refs on trace and branch conformance
nodes.summary: Replace the four call sites in `trace_branch::{trace,branch}::into_conformance_result` that emit `Err(MalformedSemanticCode { code: TRACE_EVIDENCE_TIER_OVERCLAIM })` with a typed `ConformanceError::EvidenceRefNotAdmissibleForProfile` variant whose `semantic_code()` returns the trace-specific overclaim code, so downstream code-based triagers see the diagnostic the plan promised instead of the generic malformed-code bucket.
deliverables: All four call sites updated in trace.rs / branch.rs
Tests covering `.semantic_code()` for the rejection path on both Pass and Fail input to both helpers
acceptanceCriteria: Calling `TraceCheckResult::Pass { evidence_refs: vec![FrameArtifactRef{..}] }.into_conformance_result(...)` returns an Err whose `.semantic_code()` is `TRACE_EVIDENCE_TIER_OVERCLAIM`, not `MALFORMED_SEMANTIC_CODE`.
The same is true for the branch helper.
`MalformedSemanticCode` is no longer used as a carrier for evidence-ref rejections; it goes back to signalling input-code-shape failures only.
auditFocus: Backward-compatibility of MalformedSemanticCode for its original purpose
Symmetry across the four call sites

nodes.title: Persist ingested PatchResultV02 in the itotori project repository
nodes.summary: Add durable storage for PatchResultV02 records ingested via ProjectWorkflow.ingestPatchResult so failures, output hash, and partialWrite disposition survive across CLI invocations and are joinable from the dashboard and finding ledger.
deliverables: ProjectRepository.savePatchResult (or recordPatchResultArtifact) writing patch_result rows keyed by patchResultId
ingestPatchResult wired to call the new repository method in the same actor transaction as recordFinding
Dashboard read-model surface exposing patchResult status + failureCategories
acceptanceCriteria: After ingestPatchResult, the patch result row is queryable from the DB by patchResultId with status, outputHash, and failureCategories.
retained_partial finding rows reference a patch_result row that already exists in the same transaction.
Dashboard exposes patchResult ingestion state by patchResultId.
auditFocus: Silent loss of patch result state between CLI runs
Finding referencing a patch result that was never persisted
Dashboard joins that fail because patch_result rows are missing

nodes.title: Hello-game v0.2 patch-result fixture regenerator
nodes.summary: Replace the hand-rolled deterministic hello-game v0.2 patch-result fixture with a regenerator and CI check that also cascades hashes through the alpha-vertical-proof and public manifest.
deliverables: scripts/regenerate-hello-game-patch-result-v0.2.mjs (or a just-target) that writes the expected/patch-result-v0.2.fr-FR.json from bridge + byte-size table
Hash cascade through hello-game-alpha-proof-v0.2.fr-FR.json and hello-game.manifest.json
CI check that the committed fixture matches the regenerator output
acceptanceCriteria: Running the regenerator with no source change is a no-op (idempotent).
A bridge-v0.2.json edit that adds, removes, or rehashes an asset triggers a deterministic update to all three downstream files.
CI fails if any committed file drifts from the regenerator output.
auditFocus: Hand-edited fixture drift between expected, alpha-vertical-proof, and public manifest
Asset count changes that escape the dedup-by-assetId rule
Output hash rollup hand-calculations that go stale

nodes.title: Engine-neutralize UTSUSHI-024 embed diagnostics test fixture
nodes.summary: Replace the engine-evocative `"reallive"` string in the `EmbedError::SnapshotAdapterIdMismatch` test fixture at `crates/utsushi-core/src/embed/diagnostics.rs:224` with an engine-neutral string so the audit-focus grep `'xp3|kirikiri|kag|rgss|rpgmaker|reallive|siglus'` returns empty across the whole embed module.
deliverables: One-line edit replacing `"reallive"` in the diagnostics test fixture with `"other-adapter"` (or equivalent engine-neutral string).
Confirmation grep run in the audit report verifying zero hits across `crates/utsushi-core/src/embed/`.
acceptanceCriteria: The audit-focus grep returns zero hits across `crates/utsushi-core/src/embed/`.
`cargo test -p utsushi-core embed::diagnostics` continues to pass.
auditFocus: Embed module must not reference any engine subproject by name, even in test data.
Audit-focus grep posture stays strict end-to-end.

nodes.title: Distinguish stage-time vs promote-time terminal states in PatchTransaction
nodes.summary: Add a distinct `TransactionState` variant (e.g. `StageFailed`) so callers inspecting `outcome.final_state` can distinguish a stage-time write failure (no rename ever attempted) from a promote-time rename failure (rename failed after verify), without having to re-parse the v0.2 PatchResult diagnostic list.
deliverables: New `TransactionState::StageFailed` variant added to the enum.
`record_stage_failure` updated to use the new variant.
`is_terminal` and v0.2 mappers updated to treat the new variant identically to `PromoteFailed` for wire-form compatibility.
Regression test asserting stage-write failure terminates in the new state.
acceptanceCriteria: Stage-time write failures terminate in a distinct state from promote-time rename failures.
Existing v0.2 PatchResult wire form is preserved (the new state maps to the same v0.2 category as before).
Existing `cargo test -p kaifuu-core` continues to pass.
auditFocus: New variant must not change the v0.2 wire form (no schema bump).
Existing kaifuu-reallive consumers continue to compile and pass.
Stage-time vs promote-time distinction observable from `outcome.final_state` alone.

nodes.title: Character relationship agent end-to-end coverage: recorded replay + DB round-trip + CLI snapshot
nodes.summary: Close the three end-to-end gaps deferred by ITOTORI-014: a recorded-provider fixture replay test that exercises generateCharacterRelationships against a sealed provider trace; a DB-backed persistence round-trip test (gated on DATABASE_URL) that applies migration 0031 and round-trips bios+relationships through the repository; and a CLI snapshot smoke that pins the generate-character-relationships / check-character-relationships handler text shape.
deliverables: apps/itotori/test/character-relationship-agent-recorded.test.ts exercising a committed recorded provider trace.
apps/itotori/test/character-relationship-cli-snapshot.test.ts pinning CLI text shape against a committed snapshot.
acceptanceCriteria: Recorded-provider replay asserts the bios+relationships pack matches a committed snapshot byte-for-byte.
DB round-trip applies migration 0031, persists a Fresh bio+relationship with non-empty citations, loads it back, and asserts citation arrays + source hashes are equal.

CLI snapshot asserts the human-readable output of generate-character-relationships and check-character-relationships matches committed snapshots; the test must use FakeModelProvider (no live LLM call) and must not require DATABASE_URL.
auditFocus: Recorded fixture diverges from agent's expected pack shape.
Migration 0031 schema drift caught only at live INSERT, not in CI.
CLI text-shape regression surfaces only in operator review.
